title: Windows Privesc — Vector by Priority (+ when to pivot)
body: Token privs FIRST (SeImpersonate → potato = instant SYSTEM). If absent, check service/registry/task misconfigs. If a plain Domain User with nothing modifiable → STOP local privesc and switch to domain vectors. Don't grind a workstation for SYSTEM that isn't there.
cmd:
# WINDOWS PRIVESC - token privs first, then misconfigs, then PIVOT.

# 1) TOKEN PRIVILEGES (best local path):
whoami /priv
#   SeImpersonate / SeAssignPrimaryToken ENABLED? → Potato = SYSTEM.
#   (GodPotato / PrintSpoofer / SharpEfsPotato) → see token_privs.
#   BEYOND: marcus had NEITHER - potato was OFF the table.

# 2) SERVICE / REGISTRY / TASK MISCONFIG (if no token priv):
#   - weak service perms (sc qc / accesschk)   → weak_service
#   - unquoted service path                     → unquoted_service
#   - AlwaysInstallElevated (HKCU+HKLM = 1)     → always_install_elevated
#   - writable scheduled task                   → scheduled_tasks_win
#   - DLL hijack in writable PATH dir           → win_dll_hijack
#   winPEAS/PowerUp surface these. BEYOND: none modifiable on CLIENTWK1.

# 3) STORED CREDS:  windows_creds / dpapi (registry, DPAPI, files, browser).

# 4) NO LOCAL PATH? You're a plain Domain User on a domain-joined box.
#   STOP hunting local SYSTEM. Your privesc is LATERAL/CREDENTIAL via the
#   domain → go to the pivot decision. BEYOND: marcus had no local
#   privesc, but his domain context let him kerberoast + relay to a DA.
warn: winPEAS OVER-REPORTS AD ACLs - on BEYOND it claimed marcus had GenericAll over Domain Admins, krbtgt, and the DC. That was a FALSE POSITIVE; BloodHound (which resolves effective access) showed no such edge. Never act on a winPEAS GenericAll without confirming it in BloodHound first.

choices.label: No local path — pivot to domain vectors
